input_format: nv12 at runtime, rgb at train/calibration; RAW pixels into the graph
output: seg logits, 19 Cityscapes classes; BCDL takes the argmax
licence: PIDNet upstream terms (check XuJiacong/PIDNet before redistribution)

toolchain.note: ONNX exported at opset 19 so the bilinear Resize lowers cleanly. Normalisation is NOT in the graph — the config applies it.

the_v3_fix.trap: Earlier builds calibrated on data that was NOT pre-normalised. With cal_data_type float32 the compiler's norm_type does not apply to calibration data, so the activation thresholds were gathered on a distribution the deployed model never sees.
the_v3_fix.symptom: Compiles without a single warning, loads, runs at full speed, segments to noise. No loud signal.
the_v3_fix.fix: Calibrate on pre-normalised data, produced only through common/calib_pack.py (which applies the config's data_mean_and_scale). Pair it with the no-norm ONNX so normalisation happens exactly once, in the config.
the_v3_fix.also: An even earlier build (`data_scale` 1/255, no mean subtraction) put the model on a half-normalised input — same clean-compile failure mode, different missing step.

task_metric.name: Cityscapes mIoU (19 classes)
task_metric.note: The right acceptance for a segmenter is mIoU on a labelled val set, not a cosine on the logits — a shifted-input model still emits well-formed logits. Not re-measured in this repo.

measured.note: Not re-measured here. The shipped `_v3` build is board-validated in BCDL's real-time semantic-seg path; latency lives in BCDL's benchmark results.

status: RECONSTRUCTED. The original export script was not recovered; export.py reproduces the method from the surviving config (pidnet_op19_nonorm.onnx, opset 19, raw-pixel input, data_mean_and_scale) and the upstream PIDNet-S inference forward. calib.py's pre-normalisation is the load-bearing, recoverable part and is faithful. Verify the seg-output resolution against your PIDNet checkout. Not re-run through the toolchain in this repo.
